core_mechanism: a continuous critical phase transition in a lattice of coupled two-state (binary) variables where quenched random disorder — random local biasing fields and/or random coupling strengths — competes with the ordering interaction and shifts the equilibrium critical behavior into a distinct disorder-modified universality class
brief_reason: The abstract centers on random-field and random-exchange Ising models and on characterizing their equilibrium critical behavior in the large-disorder limit, i.e. the critical phenomenon of ordering among coupled binary units under quenched random fields/couplings.
